summary: coral-wraith exfiltrates environment variables and filesystem secrets to hardcoded remote IP on install
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a chained shell command via execSync that runs `id`, enumerates `/app`, `/root`, and `/flag`, cats flag files (`/flag`, `/flag.txt`, `/app/flag.txt`, `/root/flag.txt`), greps the filesystem for `HTB{...}` strings, and dumps the full process environment via `env | sort`. The collected output is then transmitted via HTTP to the hardcoded endpoint `http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-472839` using `http.request`. The package also pulls in `child_process` at the top of the postinstall script. This is a credential/secret exfiltration payload that fires automatically on install and ships installer environment variables and filesystem contents to an attacker-controlled bare-IP host over plaintext HTTP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
